reasoning: The reference price on April 13 (which uses the April 11 close) is $484.40. Fellow financial heavyweights JPMorgan and Wells Fargo reported on Friday, April 11, both beating expectations. Notably, JPM cited 'record equities trading' and solid investment banking performance. Because GS is heavily levered to equity trading and investment banking, this proxy data strongly suggests GS will post a very strong Q1 report, acting as a positive catalyst in the near term. This pushes the short-term probability above 0.5. However, there are still significant macroeconomic headwinds, including lowered GDP guidance from key institutions and bear market concerns. Per the instructions, uncertainty increases and forecasts trend strictly toward 0.5 for the more distant maturity dates (May, July, and October).
